expectations.evidence: audit_raw.json shows structured audit output with file path and all four guardrails. The audit_report.md references the audit tool output.

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 1 (Named Range Priority) — should be PASS for this model
expectations.evidence: Guardrail 1: 'Status: PASS, Violations: 0'. 'All 14 formulas in the Calculations layer reference Named Ranges exclusively.'

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 2 (LaTeX Verification) — PASS or WARN (no complex formulas like WACC)
expectations.evidence: Guardrail 2: 'Status: PASS'. 'No complex financial formulas (WACC, NPV, DCF, IRR, etc.) were found without accompanying LaTeX verification comments.'

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 3 (Intent Notes) — should reflect comment coverage
expectations.evidence: Guardrail 3: 'Status: PASS (100% coverage)'. 'All 14 formula cells have comments attached.'

expectations.text: Reports status for Guardrail 4 (Layer Isolation) — should be PASS (no hardcoded values)
expectations.evidence: Guardrail 4: 'Status: PASS, Violations: 0'. 'No hardcoded numeric constants were found embedded in formulas.'

expectations.text: Explains what each guardrail checks in terms the user can understand
expectations.evidence: Each guardrail section includes a 'What this guardrail checks:' paragraph. E.g., G1: 'Formulas must use Named Ranges instead of cell coordinate references. Coordinate references are fragile, opaque, and break the intent-driven principle.'
